name: Confirmation Bias
one_line_hook: Why you only remember the times your gut feeling was right

name: Availability Heuristic
one_line_hook: Why you think plane crashes are more common than car crashes

name: Anchoring Bias
one_line_hook: Why the first price you see changes how much you're willing to pay

name: Sunk Cost Fallacy
one_line_hook: Why you finish bad movies just because you already paid for them

name: Dunning-Kruger Effect
one_line_hook: Why beginners are overconfident and experts are full of doubt

name: Hindsight Bias
one_line_hook: Why you say "I knew it all along" after something bad happens

name: Halo Effect
one_line_hook: Why attractive people seem smarter and more trustworthy

name: Loss Aversion
one_line_hook: Why losing $50 feels twice as bad as winning $50 feels good

name: Gambler's Fallacy
one_line_hook: Why you think a coin is "due" for heads after five tails in a row

name: Framing Effect
one_line_hook: Why "95% fat free" sells better than "contains 5% fat"

name: Fundamental Attribution Error
one_line_hook: Why you think rude drivers are bad people but you had a rough morning

name: Status Quo Bias
one_line_hook: Why you keep using the same phone brand even when another is better

name: Bandwagon Effect
one_line_hook: Why you're more likely to like a restaurant that already has a line

name: Planning Fallacy
one_line_hook: Why every project takes twice as long as you expected

name: Survivorship Bias
one_line_hook: Why you only hear success stories and never the failures

name: Optimism Bias
one_line_hook: Why you think you're less likely than average to get divorced or sick

name: Negativity Bias
one_line_hook: Why one bad review sticks with you longer than ten great ones

name: Recency Bias
one_line_hook: Why you think the stock market will keep doing whatever it just did

name: In-Group Bias
one_line_hook: Why you unconsciously trust people from your hometown more

name: Bystander Effect
one_line_hook: Why you're less likely to help someone when others are watching

name: Endowment Effect
one_line_hook: Why you'd refuse to sell your old mug for what you paid for it

name: Illusion of Control
one_line_hook: Why pressing the elevator button repeatedly makes you feel it arrives faster

name: Self-Serving Bias
one_line_hook: Why you take credit for wins but blame bad luck for losses

name: Hot Hand Fallacy
one_line_hook: Why you think a basketball player who just scored five times will score again

name: Curse of Knowledge
one_line_hook: Why experts can't explain things simply once they know too much

name: Decoy Effect
one_line_hook: Why adding a third bad option makes you choose the expensive one

name: Social Desirability Bias
one_line_hook: Why people say they exercise more than they actually do in surveys

name: Peak-End Rule
one_line_hook: Why a vacation ending badly ruins your memory of the whole trip

name: Illusory Correlation
one_line_hook: Why you notice every time a full moon coincides with something weird

name: Rosy Retrospection
one_line_hook: Why your memories of high school get better the further away you get

name: Action Bias
one_line_hook: Why goalkeepers dive even when staying still would save more shots

name: Ambiguity Effect
one_line_hook: Why you pick the option with known odds over one that might be better

name: Authority Bias
one_line_hook: Why you trust advice more when it comes from someone in a white coat

name: Automation Bias
one_line_hook: Why you trust GPS directions even when you can see the road is closed

name: Backfire Effect
one_line_hook: Why showing someone evidence they're wrong makes them believe it more

name: Barnum Effect
one_line_hook: Why horoscopes feel personally accurate even though they describe everyone

name: Base Rate Neglect
one_line_hook: Why a positive cancer test doesn't mean what you think it means

name: Bias Blind Spot
one_line_hook: Why you can spot everyone else's biases but not your own

name: Cheerleader Effect
one_line_hook: Why people look more attractive in a group photo than individually

name: Choice-Supportive Bias
one_line_hook: Why you remember your old car as better than it actually was after you sell it

name: Clustering Illusion
one_line_hook: Why you see patterns in random coin flips that aren't really there

name: Continued Influence Effect
one_line_hook: Why corrections never fully undo the damage of the original false claim

name: Contrast Effect
one_line_hook: Why an average salary sounds great after hearing a low offer first

name: Denomination Effect
one_line_hook: Why you spend a $20 bill faster than four $5 bills

name: Disposition Effect
one_line_hook: Why investors sell winning stocks too early and hold losing ones too long

name: Distinction Bias
one_line_hook: Why differences between options seem huge when comparing but tiny when choosing

name: Duration Neglect
one_line_hook: Why a 30-minute toothache feels as bad as a 3-hour one in memory

name: Empathy Gap
one_line_hook: Why you buy gym clothes when motivated but never use them a week later

name: Extrinsic Incentives Bias
one_line_hook: Why you think others only do good things for money, not because they care

name: False Consensus Effect
one_line_hook: Why you assume most people share your political views

name: False Uniqueness Effect
one_line_hook: Why you think your talents are rarer than they actually are

name: Focusing Effect
one_line_hook: Why people in California aren't actually happier despite what everyone assumes

name: Frequency Illusion
one_line_hook: Why you suddenly see your new car everywhere after you buy it

name: Hyperbolic Discounting
one_line_hook: Why you'd take $50 today over $100 next month but not $50 in a year over $100 in 13 months

name: IKEA Effect
one_line_hook: Why furniture you assembled yourself feels worth more than store-bought

name: Illusion of Asymmetric Insight
one_line_hook: Why you feel you understand your friends better than they understand you

name: Illusion of Transparency
one_line_hook: Why you feel everyone can tell you're nervous when they actually can't

name: Illusion of Validity
one_line_hook: Why interviewers trust their gut feelings about candidates despite evidence they're wrong

name: Impact Bias
one_line_hook: Why getting your dream job doesn't make you as happy as you imagined

name: Information Bias
one_line_hook: Why doctors order tests they know won't change the treatment

name: Irrational Escalation
one_line_hook: Why bidders in auctions pay way more than something is worth

name: Just-World Hypothesis
one_line_hook: Why people unconsciously blame victims for their own misfortune

name: Less-Is-Better Effect
one_line_hook: Why a small diamond ring feels more valuable than a large bag of chocolates even though the chocolates cost more

name: Leveling and Sharpening
one_line_hook: Why stories become simpler and more dramatic each time they're retold

name: Mere Exposure Effect
one_line_hook: Why you like a song more the third time you hear it even if you hated it at first

name: Money Illusion
one_line_hook: Why a 3% raise during 5% inflation feels like a win

name: Moral Credential Effect
one_line_hook: Why people who just did something good are more likely to act selfishly next

name: Naive Cynicism
one_line_hook: Why you assume others are more selfish than they actually are

name: Naive Realism
one_line_hook: Why you think people who disagree with you are uninformed or biased

name: Neglect of Probability
one_line_hook: Why you fear flying more than driving despite the statistics

name: Normalcy Bias
one_line_hook: Why people stand still during disasters instead of evacuating

name: Not-Invented-Here Bias
one_line_hook: Why teams reject perfectly good solutions just because they didn't create them

name: Omission Bias
one_line_hook: Why doing nothing feels less guilty than taking action, even if both cause harm

name: Ostrich Effect
one_line_hook: Why people check their bank balance less when they know they've overspent

name: Outcome Bias
one_line_hook: Why you judge a decision as bad just because it turned out badly

name: Overconfidence Effect
one_line_hook: Why 93% of drivers think they're above average behind the wheel

name: Pareidolia
one_line_hook: Why you see faces in clouds, toast, and random wood grain

name: Pessimism Bias
one_line_hook: Why depressed people often make more accurate predictions about the future

name: Pro-Innovation Bias
one_line_hook: Why tech founders overestimate how quickly the world will adopt their invention

name: Projection Bias
one_line_hook: Why you grocery-shop hungry and buy ten things you don't actually want

name: Pseudocertainty Effect
one_line_hook: Why people accept sure small gains but gamble to avoid certain losses

name: Reactance
one_line_hook: Why being told you can't do something makes you want to do it more

name: Restraint Bias
one_line_hook: Why people overestimate their ability to resist temptation and put themselves in front of it

name: Rhyme-as-Reason Effect
one_line_hook: Why "if it doesn't fit, you must acquit" was more persuasive than a plain argument

name: Risk Compensation
one_line_hook: Why adding safety equipment sometimes makes people take more dangerous risks

name: Scarcity Heuristic
one_line_hook: Why "limited time offer" makes you want something you didn't care about before

name: Selective Perception
one_line_hook: Why two people watching the same argument walk away with different versions

name: Semmelweis Reflex
one_line_hook: Why new ideas get rejected by experts before any evidence is examined

name: Shared Information Bias
one_line_hook: Why group meetings waste time discussing what everyone already knows

name: Social Comparison Bias
one_line_hook: Why managers unconsciously hire candidates weaker than themselves

name: Spotlight Effect
one_line_hook: Why you think everyone noticed you spilled coffee on your shirt

name: Stereotyping
one_line_hook: Why first impressions based on group membership override actual evidence

name: Subadditivity Effect
one_line_hook: Why people estimate the parts of a thing add up to less than the whole

name: Telescoping Effect
one_line_hook: Why recent events feel older and old events feel more recent than they are

name: Texas Sharpshooter Fallacy
one_line_hook: Why people find patterns in random data by looking at the data first

name: Time-Saving Bias
one_line_hook: Why people think speeding from 40mph to 50mph saves more time than from 90 to 100

name: Triviality Bias
one_line_hook: Why committees spend more time debating coffee choices than the annual budget

name: Unit Bias
one_line_hook: Why you feel compelled to finish whatever portion you're given regardless of size

name: Well-Travelled Road Effect
one_line_hook: Why your daily commute always feels shorter than an unfamiliar route of the same length

name: Worse-Than-Average Effect
one_line_hook: Why people think they're worse than average at tasks they've never tried

name: Zero-Risk Bias
one_line_hook: Why people prefer eliminating a small risk completely over reducing a large risk significantly

name: Zero-Sum Bias
one_line_hook: Why people assume that if someone else gains, they must be losing

name: Acquiescence Bias
one_line_hook: Why people tend to agree with survey questions regardless of their actual opinion

name: Affect Heuristic
one_line_hook: Why you judge risks as lower for things you enjoy doing

name: Agent Detection
one_line_hook: Why you suspect someone moved your keys even when you just misplaced them

name: Apophenia
one_line_hook: Why you see meaningful connections between completely unrelated things

name: Belief Bias
one_line_hook: Why you accept a bad argument if its conclusion matches what you already believe

name: Ben Franklin Effect
one_line_hook: Why asking someone for a small favor makes them like you more, not less

name: Caring Effect
one_line_hook: Why people perform better when they know someone cares about the outcome

name: Cashless Effect
one_line_hook: Why you spend more freely when paying by card than with physical cash

name: Category Size Bias
one_line_hook: Why you assume there are more people in a country just because it has a large population

name: Cognitive Dissonance
one_line_hook: Why smokers convince themselves their habit isn't that dangerous

name: Competitive Arousal
one_line_hook: Why people at auctions get swept up and pay far more than they planned

name: Conservatism Bias
one_line_hook: Why people update their beliefs too slowly when new evidence arrives

name: Context Effect
one_line_hook: Why the same food tastes better in an expensive restaurant

name: Congruence Bias
one_line_hook: Why scientists keep testing their own hypothesis instead of trying to disprove it

name: Courtesy Bias
one_line_hook: Why customer satisfaction surveys almost always skew positive

name: Cryptomnesia
one_line_hook: Why you sometimes think you invented an idea you actually heard years ago

name: Cultural Bias
one_line_hook: Why what counts as polite, punctual, or generous differs by country

name: Default Effect
one_line_hook: Why organ donation rates vary wildly depending on whether it's opt-in or opt-out

name: Defensive Attribution
one_line_hook: Why people who are similar to accident victims receive less blame

name: Professional Deformation
one_line_hook: Why a hammer-wielding handyman sees every problem as a nail

name: Egocentric Bias
one_line_hook: Why each person in a group believes they contributed more than their fair share

name: Emotional Cascade
one_line_hook: Why ruminating on a small frustration can spiral into a full breakdown

name: Escalation of Commitment
one_line_hook: Why companies keep funding failing projects because of how much they've already spent

name: Psychological Essentialism
one_line_hook: Why people believe there's a hidden "essence" that makes categories real

name: Experimenter Bias
one_line_hook: Why researchers subtly influence results to confirm what they expected to find

name: Extension Neglect
one_line_hook: Why people donate the same amount to save 2000 birds as to save 200000

name: Extreme Aversion
one_line_hook: Why restaurants put a $300 steak on the menu to make the $80 option feel reasonable

name: Face-ism
one_line_hook: Why men are photographed more from the face up while women are shown more of their body

name: Familiarity Heuristic
one_line_hook: Why you trust your home country's brands over foreign ones even when quality is identical

name: Feature Creep
one_line_hook: Why apps keep adding features until they're too complicated for anyone to use

name: Fluency Heuristic
one_line_hook: Why stocks with easy-to-pronounce names outperform ones with complex names

name: Forer Effect
one_line_hook: Why personality tests feel scarily accurate even when everyone gets the same result

name: Functional Fixedness
one_line_hook: Why you can't see that a butter knife would work perfectly as a screwdriver

name: Google Effect
one_line_hook: Why you forget information you know you can easily look up online

name: Groupthink
one_line_hook: Why smart teams make bad decisions when everyone is afraid to speak up

name: Hard-Easy Effect
one_line_hook: Why you're overconfident on hard questions and underconfident on easy ones

name: Identifiable Victim Effect
one_line_hook: Why one named child raises more charity money than statistics about millions

name: Illusion of Explanatory Depth
one_line_hook: Why you think you understand how a toilet works until you try to explain it

name: Imagination Inflation
one_line_hook: Why vividly imagining an event can make you believe it actually happened to you

name: Inattentional Blindness
one_line_hook: Why you can miss a gorilla walking through a scene you're watching intently

name: Intention-Action Gap
one_line_hook: Why people who say they'll start exercising Monday almost never do

name: Introspection Illusion
one_line_hook: Why you think you know your own motivations when you mostly don't

name: Primacy Effect
one_line_hook: Why the first item on a menu or ballot gets chosen more often

name: Law of Small Numbers
one_line_hook: Why you trust a study of 20 people almost as much as one with 20000

name: Look-Elsewhere Effect
one_line_hook: Why scientists testing many hypotheses at once are bound to find a false positive

name: Miller's Law
one_line_hook: Why phone numbers were originally 7 digits — the limit of working memory

name: McNamara Fallacy
one_line_hook: Why optimizing for what's measurable often ignores what actually matters

name: Mental Accounting
one_line_hook: Why you'd never take $50 from savings but happily spend a $50 bonus on junk

name: Mere Ownership Effect
one_line_hook: Why you rate objects higher just because you own them

name: Misinformation Effect
one_line_hook: Why a leading question from police can permanently alter a witness's memory

name: Modality Effect
one_line_hook: Why you remember the last few items in a list better when you hear them than read them

name: Moral Luck
one_line_hook: Why two identical drunk drivers are judged very differently if one hits someone

name: Moral Licensing
one_line_hook: Why people who recycle feel more entitled to splurge on a flight

name: Motivational Salience
one_line_hook: Why hungry people notice food-related words in a crowded word search faster

name: Narrative Fallacy
one_line_hook: Why we can't help turning random events into a story with a villain and a hero

name: Next-in-Line Effect
one_line_hook: Why you forget what others said right before your turn to speak

name: Noble Edge Effect
one_line_hook: Why consumers prefer products from companies they see as ethical

name: Outgroup Homogeneity Bias
one_line_hook: Why "they" all seem the same but "we" are all unique individuals

name: Overattribution Effect
one_line_hook: Why you assume someone is generous because of who they are, not just what they did

name: Picture Superiority Effect
one_line_hook: Why you remember images far better than words you read

name: Placebo Effect
one_line_hook: Why a sugar pill can genuinely reduce pain if the patient believes it will

name: Post-Purchase Rationalization
one_line_hook: Why you suddenly think of all the reasons your expensive buy was actually smart

name: Present Bias
one_line_hook: Why you choose Netflix tonight over the report that's due tomorrow

name: Processing Difficulty Effect
one_line_hook: Why hard-to-read fonts sometimes make people think more carefully

name: Proportionality Bias
one_line_hook: Why people assume big events must have big causes — not random accidents

name: Regressive Thinking
one_line_hook: Why you revert to childlike reasoning when stressed or threatened

name: Pygmalion Effect
one_line_hook: Why students perform better when teachers genuinely expect them to succeed

name: Reactive Devaluation
one_line_hook: Why a peace proposal sounds better before you find out your enemy suggested it

name: Reminiscence Bump
one_line_hook: Why the music from your teenage years always sounds better than anything new

name: Repetition Blindness
one_line_hook: Why readers miss a repeated word when it appears at the end of one line and the start of the next

name: Reputation Trap
one_line_hook: Why high-performing employees get stuck in roles they've outgrown

name: Salience Bias
one_line_hook: Why the loudest voice in a meeting gets remembered as the most important

name: Self-Consistency Bias
one_line_hook: Why your memory edits your past opinions to match who you are today

name: Self-Handicapping
one_line_hook: Why students party the night before a test so failure has a built-in excuse

name: Self-Interest Bias
one_line_hook: Why people rate their own performance as above average in salary negotiations

name: Serial Position Effect
one_line_hook: Why you remember the first and last things on a list but forget the middle

name: Sleeper Effect
one_line_hook: Why persuasion from a low-credibility source actually increases over time

name: Social Loafing
one_line_hook: Why people pull a rope harder alone than as part of a team

name: System Justification
one_line_hook: Why even disadvantaged groups defend the system that disadvantages them

name: Source Monitoring Error
one_line_hook: Why you forget whether you read something in the news or dreamed it

name: Spacing Effect
one_line_hook: Why cramming the night before works worse than studying a little each day

name: Interpreter Module Bias
one_line_hook: Why your brain fabricates logical explanations for things you did instinctively

name: Story Bias
one_line_hook: Why a single anecdote beats a spreadsheet full of data every time

name: Subjective Validation
one_line_hook: Why two contradictory statements both feel true if you want them to apply to you

name: Suggestibility
one_line_hook: Why therapists can accidentally implant false childhood memories in patients

name: Thinking Fast and Slow
one_line_hook: Why your gut answers a math question before your brain even starts working

name: Third-Person Effect
one_line_hook: Why you think advertising manipulates other people but never yourself

name: Tip-of-the-Tongue Phenomenon
one_line_hook: Why you can feel a word exists but can't quite pull it out of your memory

name: Tragedy of the Commons
one_line_hook: Why shared resources get destroyed even when everyone knows it's happening

name: Trait Ascription Bias
one_line_hook: Why you see yourself as flexible but others as stubborn and predictable

name: Tunnel Vision Bias
one_line_hook: Why investigators sometimes ignore evidence that points away from their prime suspect

name: Ultimate Attribution Error
one_line_hook: Why outgroup failures are blamed on character but successes on luck

name: Scope Insensitivity
one_line_hook: Why you donate nearly the same amount to save 2000 birds as 200000

name: Verbatim Effect
one_line_hook: Why you remember the gist of a conversation but forget the exact words almost immediately

name: Visceral Bias
one_line_hook: Why a disgusting smell in a room makes people judge moral transgressions more harshly

name: Von Restorff Effect
one_line_hook: Why the one yellow item in a row of black ones is always remembered

name: Weapon Focus Effect
one_line_hook: Why eyewitnesses who see a gun can't reliably describe the person holding it

name: Weber-Fechner Law
one_line_hook: Why saving $10 on a $20 item feels great but not on a $10000 item

name: Wishful Thinking
one_line_hook: Why fans genuinely believe their team will win before a game, not just hope

name: Word Superiority Effect
one_line_hook: Why you recognize a letter faster when it's inside a word than standing alone

name: Cheerfulness Bias
one_line_hook: Why people rate their life satisfaction higher on sunny days without realizing it

name: Zeigarnik Effect
one_line_hook: Why unfinished tasks keep nagging at your brain but completed ones disappear

name: Round Number Bias
one_line_hook: Why prices ending in .99 sell more even though everyone knows the trick

name: Regression to the Mean
one_line_hook: Why praising a great performance and criticizing a bad one both seem to "work"

name: Attribute Substitution
one_line_hook: Why you answer a hard question by secretly replacing it with an easier one

name: Attentional Bias
one_line_hook: Why people in pain notice pain-related words faster than neutral words

name: Blind Spot Bias
one_line_hook: Why smart people are especially good at explaining away their own biases

name: Bounded Rationality
one_line_hook: Why people pick "good enough" options rather than the theoretically best one

name: Temporal Discounting
one_line_hook: Why buy-now-pay-later schemes make people spend 20% more on average

name: Causal Attribution Error
one_line_hook: Why you think your city's rain is caused by something local, not global weather

name: Commitment and Consistency Bias
one_line_hook: Why people who sign a petition are far more likely to donate later

name: Conceptual Consumption
one_line_hook: Why thinking about eating a food can satisfy some of the craving

name: Conjunction Fallacy
one_line_hook: Why a specific story feels more probable than a general one even when it can't be

name: Consistency Bias
one_line_hook: Why you misremember your past opinions as always matching your present ones

name: Construal Level Theory
one_line_hook: Why things feel more feasible the closer they are in time or space

name: Contagion Effect
one_line_hook: Why people won't drink from a glass stirred by a cockroach even after washing it

name: Cue-Dependent Forgetting
one_line_hook: Why you forget what you were going to say the moment you enter a new room

name: Current Moment Bias
one_line_hook: Why people consistently underestimate how much they'll change in the next decade

name: Declinism
one_line_hook: Why every generation believes the world is worse than it used to be

name: Effort Heuristic
one_line_hook: Why you rate a poem as better when told the poet spent months on it

name: End-of-History Illusion
one_line_hook: Why you think who you are right now is finally the finished version of yourself

name: Evening Effect
one_line_hook: Why willpower and good decisions tend to deteriorate as the day goes on

name: Expectation Bias
one_line_hook: Why wine tastes better when you're told it's expensive even if it isn't

name: False Memory
one_line_hook: Why people remember events that never actually happened with complete confidence

name: Foot-in-the-Door Technique
one_line_hook: Why agreeing to wear a small button makes you more likely to put a billboard in your yard

name: Generation Effect
one_line_hook: Why you remember things better when you figure them out yourself than when you're told

name: Illusory Truth Effect
one_line_hook: Why repeating a false claim enough times makes people start to believe it

name: Magical Thinking
one_line_hook: Why athletes wear lucky socks and never wash them during a winning streak

name: Number Anchoring
one_line_hook: Why asking if Gandhi died before or after age 144 changes your estimate of his age

name: Locus of Control Bias
one_line_hook: Why some people believe luck runs their life while others feel fully in charge

name: Mum Effect
one_line_hook: Why employees don't tell their bosses about problems they know about

name: Name-Letter Effect
one_line_hook: Why people subtly prefer their own initials in brand names and license plates

name: Nostalgia Bias
one_line_hook: Why the decade you grew up in always feels like the best era for music

name: Pratfall Effect
one_line_hook: Why making a small mistake makes competent people more likable, not less

name: Priming Effect
one_line_hook: Why seeing money on a desk makes people less likely to ask for help minutes later

name: Recency vs Primacy
one_line_hook: Why what happens first and last in a job interview matters more than the middle

name: Representativeness Heuristic
one_line_hook: Why a quiet bookish person seems more likely to be a librarian than a farmer despite the odds

name: Role Suction
one_line_hook: Why even volunteers playing prison guards start acting cruel within days

name: Satisficing
one_line_hook: Why people pick the first good-enough option instead of searching for the best one

name: Signal Detection Bias
one_line_hook: Why doctors miss rare diseases and security guards flag innocent people

name: Simulation Heuristic
one_line_hook: Why near-misses feel worse than disasters that were clearly inevitable

name: Social Facilitation
one_line_hook: Why you ride a bike faster when others are watching but freeze during a speech

name: Temporal Self-Appraisal
one_line_hook: Why your past self seems more embarrassing as time goes on but not your future self

name: Financial Ostrich Effect
one_line_hook: Why investors check their portfolios less during market crashes

name: Truth Effect
one_line_hook: Why hearing a statement multiple times makes you rate it as more truthful

name: Two-Factor Theory of Emotion
one_line_hook: Why your heart racing can be mistaken for attraction if an attractive person is nearby

name: Unskilled and Unaware Effect
one_line_hook: Why the least competent people are often the most confident in their ability

name: Variable Reward Effect
one_line_hook: Why slot machines and social media feeds are more addictive than predictable rewards

name: Winner-Take-All Bias
one_line_hook: Why talent and effort matter less than timing and luck for who ends up famous
